title: Add upgrade and asset CLI parser scaffolding
description: Extend CLI argument parsing and command dispatch to register `takt upgrade --dry-run` and `takt asset {mark-owned,unmark-owned,list}` entry points, with command stubs routed through `cli.py`.
acceptance_criteria: `build_parser()` accepts `upgrade --dry-run` and `asset` subcommands with the required sub-subcommands.
`main()` dispatches upgrade and asset invocations through dedicated command functions.
Existing command wiring remains unchanged for unrelated CLI commands.

handoff_summary.completed:
- Added `upgrade` subparser with `--dry-run` flag to `build_parser()`
- Added `asset` subparser with `mark-owned`, `unmark-owned`, `list` sub-subcommands to `build_parser()`
- Added `command_upgrade()` stub in `cli.py`
- Added `command_asset()` stub in `cli.py`
- Wired both commands into `main()` dispatch (before `make_services()` call, mirroring `init` pattern)
- Verified syntax with `py_compile` and all `--help` outputs
handoff_summary.remaining: Implementation of actual upgrade logic (`command_upgrade`) and asset management logic (`command_asset`) — these are stubs that return exit 1 until a downstream bead provides the real implementation by filling in the function bodies and adding helpers in `onboarding.py`.
handoff_summary.risks: The stub command functions return exit code 1 with an error message when invoked. Downstream beads that implement the logic must replace the stub bodies in `cli.py` with real implementation. The `command_upgrade` and `command_asset` signatures use `(args, console)` only — if the downstream bead needs `storage`, the signature will need to change and `main()` dispatch updated accordingly.
handoff_summary.next_action: A downstream bead should implement the bodies of `command_upgrade` and `command_asset` in `cli.py` and add `write_assets_manifest`, `read_assets_manifest`, `merge_config_keys` helpers to `onboarding.py`.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None. This bead only appended to `cli.py` — added parser entries after `init_parser` and new functions before `main()`. No existing code paths were modified. The `main()` dispatch added two `if` branches that short-circuit before `make_services()`, identical to the existing `init` pattern. No overlap with other in-progress beads.
handoff_summary.design_decisions:
1. Command stubs live entirely in `cli.py` (not delegating to `onboarding.py` at this stage) because the spec places `command_upgrade` and `command_asset` in `cli.py`, and `onboarding.py` helpers do not yet exist. Importing from a non-existent module at call-time would produce an opaque `ImportError`; the stub-with-error-message pattern is friendlier and consistent with how an unimplemented command should behave.

2. `upgrade` and `asset` are dispatched before `make_services()` in `main()` — same pattern as `init`. The upgrade command needs to work on a project that may not have a fully-initialized `.takt/` bead store (e.g., upgrading a partially-initialised project), so it should not require `RepositoryStorage` to be constructed.

3. `asset_command` is `required=True` on the asset sub-subparser, so `takt asset` without a subcommand produces a usage error rather than silently falling through.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes:
Tester should verify:
- `takt upgrade --help` shows `--dry-run` flag
- `takt asset --help` shows `mark-owned`, `unmark-owned`, `list` subcommands
- `takt asset mark-owned --help` shows `glob` positional argument
- `takt asset unmark-owned --help` shows `glob` positional argument
- `takt asset list --help` shows no required args
- `takt upgrade` (invoked) exits 1 with error message (stub)
- `takt asset mark-owned foo` (invoked) exits 1 with error message (stub)
- Existing commands (`plan`, `run`, `bead`, `init`, etc.) are unaffected
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The stub command functions return exit 1 with an error message. They are not no-ops — any CI pipeline that calls `takt upgrade` will see a failure until the implementation bead completes. The command signatures use `(args, console)` without `storage`; if the downstream implementation needs `RepositoryStorage`, the signature and `main()` dispatch must be updated.

conflict_risks: None. This bead only appended to `cli.py` — added parser entries after `init_parser` and new functions before `main()`. No existing code paths were modified. The `main()` dispatch added two `if` branches that short-circuit before `make_services()`, identical to the existing `init` pattern. No overlap with other in-progress beads.

execution_history.summary: Added `takt upgrade --dry-run` and `takt asset {mark-owned,unmark-owned,list}` parser entries and command stubs to `cli.py`. Both commands are wired into `main()` before `make_services()` (same pattern as `init`). Command functions contain docstrings describing the intended behavior and return exit code 1 with an error message until the downstream implementation bead fills them in.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
